title: Quantum Fluctuations in the Quantum Hall Effect
abstract:
Quantum fluctuations in the Hall conductivity, σxy, in two-dimensional systems of mesoscopic sizes in magnetic fields are studied. The fluctuation is shown to exhibit distinct features in the quantum Hall regime. Specifically, numerical results for finite lattice systems with disorder show that fluctuations in σxy, whose root-mean-square magnitude, δ σxy is of the order of e
                  2/h independent of sample size, has a definite relation with the averaged value, <σxy>, in which δ σxy decreases sharply as <σxy> approaches the quantised value. Temperature effects are also discussed.
